items.question: Manifest vs lockfile — what's the difference?
items.answer: The manifest (requirements.txt/pyproject.toml) holds the loose constraints you author; the lockfile holds the exact, fully-resolved versions (direct + transitive, often with hashes) the resolver produced. You edit the manifest; you generate and commit the lock.

items.question: Why do builds still drift if you only pin direct dependencies?
items.answer: Because transitive dependencies (the deps of your deps) remain unpinned and can resolve to newer versions over time. A lockfile pins the whole tree.

items.question: What breaks if the lockfile is stale after editing the manifest?
items.answer: Installs follow the lock, so they ignore your new manifest intent — you get old versions, or a deploy check fails because the lock no longer matches the manifest.
